gpt55_review_summary: Hume the 2nd found stale mutable-URI cache keying, missing reset_cache clearing, and missing release launch-config plumbing. The amended branch keys cached loads by hashed connector-action content identity, requires copy content_hash values when caching is enabled, clears the payload LRU on reset_cache, plumbs the release launch-config builder/CLI, and Hume rechecked those findings as clean.
verification: poetry run pytest tests/test_vllm_kv_injection_vllm_native_provider.py tests/test_vllm_kv_injection_vllm_transfer_config.py tests/test_vllm_smoke.py tests/test_databricks_vllm_smoke_job.py tests/test_engine_launch_config.py tests/test_transformers_generator.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py -q -> 181 passed, 2 skipped
poetry run python -m compileall -q src tests -> passed
poetry check -> passed with existing duplicated project/tool.poetry metadata warnings
changed-file path-only secret scan for Databricks token/profile strings -> no matches
what_changed: Added an opt-in, byte-bounded vLLM native-provider payload URI cache keyed by content-bearing connector-action identity and cleared by vLLM reset_cache.
Plumbed document_kv.payload_cache_max_bytes through vLLM transfer config, release launch-config sidecars/CLI, local smoke launch metadata, and Databricks smoke job parameters.
Made the Transformers benchmark handoff generator populate KV payload content hashes so cached URI loads have immutable content identity.
why: The latest g6/L4 Databricks run proved the native connector path works but showed UC Volume payload materialization dominates latency; repeated-document benchmarks need a safe hot-payload cache before another meaningful Databricks run.
